headline: Pakistan POST-BAND-CROSSING WATCH cycle 1 — first cycle after May 12 band crossing (Developing -> Critical at 17.5). Active NDMA pre-monsoon weather window (May 13-17, most active May 13-15) covering Punjab, KPK, Balochistan, GB, AJK. May 10-11 precursor storms killed 4 (1 child, 1 woman, 2 men) and injured 16 (7 children, 1 woman, 8 men) in KPK (Mardan, Swabi, Malakand, Shangla). NDMA forecasts 2026 full monsoon 22-26% above normal — highest alert in recent years. PM-approved emergency preparedness plan (NDMA Strategic Plan, May 7). No mass-casualty event has yet materialised in the May 13 window itself; precursor casualty data is already in scoring window from May 12 band-crossing assessment. EMP/ACT carry-forward credit notation: NDMA pre-monsoon alert + PM-approved preparedness plan represent functional anticipatory-awareness infrastructure (A5 anchor 3) — partially offsets recent HRCP-evidence drag. Per first-post-band-crossing protocol: composite re-pinned at 17.5 Critical with explicit watch open through May 17. No score change. Boundary-case protocol active: 17.5 is 2.5 below Developing floor (20.0), 17.5 above Critical floor (0.0). If mass-casualty event materialises May 14-17, EMP dimension downgrade possible in next cycle.
band_path_note: Published canonical countries.json value remains 20.3. The May 12 band-crossing proposal moved to 17.5 Critical and is currently pending review. This May 13 cycle confirms the May 12 17.5 pinpoint with no further downward pressure within the May 13 in-window evidence set. If May 12 proposal is approved, both published-canonical (20.3) and active-pending (17.5) need to be tracked. The 2.8-point delta vs canonical published value is sub-5pt threshold but the band-crossing is material.
boundary_case_note: Proposed composite 17.5 sits within the Critical band (0-20), 2.5 below the Developing floor (20.0). Within 1pt of band threshold? No — 2.5 distance from band threshold so technically outside the 1pt boundary-case window. However, post-band-crossing watch protocol active.
watch_reason: NDMA pre-monsoon active weather window May 13-17 (most active May 13-15). Mass-casualty/displacement events in window could compound to additional EMP/ACT downward pressure in next cycle.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.875 sustained from May 12 band crossing. A5 Anticipatory Awareness: NDMA pre-monsoon alert and PM-approved preparedness plan are anchor-3 functional infrastructure — partial positive offset noted but does not move dimension off May 12 conservative pin. A1 Suffering Detection: HRCP and COIED mechanisms exist but produce undercounts.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.875 sustained from May 12. No new EMP-relevant evidence in window. Religious-minority and refugee mistreatment patterns continue from May 12 record.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.875 sustained from May 12. AC1 Responsiveness: NDMA preparedness plan represents structural responsiveness infrastructure (positive offset notation). No mass-casualty-response failure in window. AC4 Resource Mobilization: PM-approved emergency plan is positive structural signal but untested in current window.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.375 sustained from May 12. Ahmadi minority exclusion, Afghan refugee expulsion, GBV scale (6,500+; 145/148 gender gap) continue.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.875 sustained from May 12. Afghan refugee coerced expulsion conduct continues from May 12 record.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.375 sustained from May 12. State-side response to HRCP report remains non-action/denial.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.875 sustained from May 12. Executive expansion and judicial weakening structural trajectory continues.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.375 sustained from May 12. Constitutional protections vs. expanding executive power consistency gap continues.

key_evidence: May 12 2026 (in-window, NDMA): Pre-monsoon weather alert for May 13-17 covering Punjab, KPK, Balochistan, GB, AJK; most active period May 13-15.
May 10-11 2026 (carry-over from May 12 assessment): Storms killed 4 and injured 16 in KPK (Mardan, Swabi, Malakand, Shangla).
May 12 2026 (in-window, NDMA): 2026 full monsoon forecast 22-26% above normal — highest alert in recent years.
May 7 2026 (anchor): NDMA Strategic Plan to Combat Monsoon Flood Risks approved by PM — preparedness infrastructure documented.

Background evidence base (May 12 band-crossing): HRCP 2025 annual report; 273+ enforced disappearances; 531,700 Afghan refugees coerced; Ahmadi religious-minority violence; GBV/gender-gap data; HRW 2026 World Report on crackdown; Genocide Watch country report.
Indus Waters Treaty suspended by India since April 2025 — water security affecting 240M+ people remains unresolved.
India-Pakistan ceasefire holding one year on (regional stability positive anchor).

evidence.source: The Watchers — NDMA warns GLOF risk after deadly storms hit Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan
evidence.finding: 4 killed (1 child, 1 woman, 2 men), 16 injured (7 children, 1 woman, 8 men) in KPK May 10-11 precursor storms. NDMA pre-monsoon alert active May 13-17. GLOF risk in northern catchments.

evidence.source: Express Tribune — Pakistan's 2026 monsoon season will be 26% wetter: NDMA
evidence.finding: NDMA forecasts 2026 monsoon 22-26% above normal — highest alert in recent years. Anticipatory awareness signal.

evidence.source: Daily Pakistan — Pakistan Launches Strategic Plan to Combat Monsoon Flood Risks Ahead of 2026
evidence.finding: PM-approved emergency preparedness plan May 7. Structural anticipatory action.

evidence.source: Pakistan Today — HRCP report flags shrinking freedoms, rising violence in Pakistan in 2025
evidence.finding: Carry-over from May 12 band-crossing evidence base: comprehensive HRCP documentation of multi-dimension deterioration.
Crackdown on media, opposition, civil society continues. Carry-over anchor evidence.

math_check.rounding_correction_note: Math gives 17.2; reported 17.5 follows May 12 conservative-rounding precedent. Both within Critical band (0-20).
math_check.validator_check: Sub-5pt threshold delta sustained from May 12. Band-crossing already on record from May 12 proposal. Post-band-crossing watch cycle 1 — no further downward movement; no further upward movement. Conservative re-pin at 17.5 Critical.

carry_forward_credits.anticipatory_awareness_offset: +0.25 sub-dimension equivalent on AWR/A5 anchor (NDMA pre-monsoon alert + PM-approved preparedness plan). Sub-threshold within May 12 conservative dimensional pins. Not applied to composite. Will be re-evaluated in next cycle if no mass-casualty event materialises (positive offset confirmation) or absorbed into negative pressure if event materialises (offset cancelled).

negativeSignals: Active pre-monsoon weather window May 13-17 with elevated GLOF risk; 4 already killed in precursor storms; HRCP-documented multi-dimensional deterioration sustained; Afghan refugee expulsion conduct continues; Ahmadi minority targeting continues; expanding executive power; constitutional protections erosion.
positiveSignals: NDMA pre-monsoon alert system functional; PM-approved emergency preparedness plan (May 7); 2026 monsoon forecast issued in advance (22-26% above normal — anticipatory awareness signal); India-Pakistan ceasefire holding one year; COIED domestic accountability mechanism formally exists (though produces undercount); HRCP itself functions as domestic transparency mechanism.
sector_context: South Asia / climate-vulnerability + civic-space cluster. First post-band-crossing watch cycle. Pakistan is the largest entity to cross into Critical band in 2026 by population (240M+). Cross-reference: India-Pakistan ceasefire structural; IWT suspension unresolved.
open_actions_resolved: No further downward movement in window
open_actions_carried: Watch: NDMA situation reports May 14-17 for mass-casualty/displacement events
Watch: any state response to HRCP report
Watch: Sulyok dismissal not applicable (Hungary item)
methodology_consistency_check: Post-band-crossing watch protocol applied. Composite re-pinned at May 12 17.5 conservative value. No new in-window mass-casualty event triggers further downward movement. Anticipatory-awareness positive offset noted as sub-threshold (not applied to composite). Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
